title: Contract Tests, Race and Leak Checks, Chaos, and Release · Language Lens: Layered Test Strategy
summary: A Go 1.26 service team must layer pure tests, fuzzing, race and leak checks, disposable-broker faults, compatible rollout, upgrade, rollback, and recovery proof; reviewers reject hidden goroutine ownership, unbounded work, stale channel state, and claims that stop at a nil client error. The learner predicts values, ownership transitions, and stop paths before seeing a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the layered test strategy increment in a production Go messaging defense dossier. Learne…
objectives: Test pure policy, envelope schema, topology routes, publisher confirms, consumer effects, disposable broker integration, and production probes at distinct layers; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Define availability, acceptance, routing, processing latency, age, loss, duplicate, poison, recovery, and capacity SLOs with symptom and cause alerts; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Predict Layered Test Strategy · Theory Layered Test Strategy
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go messaging team handles case 1036 during confirm-window load test with broker flow control. Build deterministic Go evidence to gate release on contracts, race and leak evidence, broker faults, compatibility, rollback, and upgrade path; reject stale connection and channel generations; then lose a quorum member and recompute majority availability. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Test pure policy, envelope schema, topology routes, publisher confirms, consumer effects, disposable broker integration, and production probes at distinct layers; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
